title: First Report of Kosakonia cowanii as the Causal Agent of Bacterial Wilt Disease in Banana (Musa paradisiaca L.) in Golobilas Village, West Manggarai Regency, East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia
author: Graciella Luise Laoth
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Banana (Musa paradisiaca L.) is an important food commodity in West Manggarai Regency, contributing significantly to local food security. However, banana production declined by 14.31% between 2022 and 2025, which is suspected to be caused by bacterial wilt disease. This disease is commonly associated with the Ralstonia solanacearum species complex, which infects the xylem tissue, disrupts water and nutrient transport, and ultimately leads to plant wilting and death. This study aimed to identify the bacterial pathogen causing wilt disease in banana plants in West Manggarai Regency using molecular methods based on the Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) technique. The molecular analysis revealed that the causal agent of the disease was Kosakonia cowanii, a bacterium known as an opportunistic pathogen. This finding indicates that bacterial wilt in banana is not exclusively caused by Ralstonia spp., but may also involve other bacterial species capable of acting as pathogens under certain conditions. The results of this study provide scientific information that can serve as a basis for developing effective, targeted, and sustainable disease management strategies in West Manggarai Regency.

keywords: banana, bacterial wilt, Kosakonia cowanii, PCR, opportunistic pathogen, West Manggarai
